content:
6 min read
Just now

--

Anthropic shipped them June 18 to Team and Enterprise: one live, self-updating page per session — plus 3 jobs it still can’t do.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Vitaly Gariev on UnsplashOn June 18, Claude Code stopped replying with a wall of text and started replying with a web page. Ask it to investigate an incident or walk a reviewer through a pull request, and it now builds a live, shareable page — a timeline, a dashboard, a checklist — out of the session you just ran, and republishes it in place as the work continues.
Anthropic calls the feature artifacts. The more useful description: the thing an engineer hands the rest of the team is no longer a paragraph in Slack. It’s a link that stays current.
What actually shipped
The mechanics are narrow, and worth stating precisely. A Claude Code session — a refactor, an incident dig, an analysis of months of logs — can now be published as an interactive page. Anthropic says Claude Code builds it from the full context of your session: your codebase, your connected tools, and the conversation itself. You don’t wire up a data source or stand up a dashboard server. You ask for a page; it assembles one from what already exists.
Then it keeps it alive. When the session updates the page, the open page refreshes in place and everyone holding the link…
